findings.name: SQL Injection
findings.description: Parameter 'id' on GET http://127.0.0.1:5050/sqli?id=1 is vulnerable to SQL injection. The application reflects database-level errors or differential responses when SQL metacharacters are sent.
findings.evidence:
You have an error in your SQL syntax
---
You have an error in your SQL syntax; check the manual for MySQL server
findings.remediation: Use parameterized queries / prepared statements with bound parameters. Validate and whitelist input where possible. Apply least-privilege database accounts and disable verbose error pages in production.

findings.name: Reflected XSS
findings.description: Reflected XSS: parameter 'q' on GET http://127.0.0.1:5050/search?q=%3Cscript%3Ealert%28%27vsx0106af00%27%29%3C%2Fscript%3E reflects the supplied payload in the response without proper encoding. An attacker can execute JavaScript in the victim's browser context.
findings.remediation: Apply context-aware output encoding (HTML, attribute, JavaScript, URL). Use a strict Content-Security-Policy. Validate and reject input containing HTML/JS where it is not expected. Frameworks like React/Angular auto-escape - avoid using innerHTML / dangerouslySetInnerHTML.

findings.name: Reflected XSS
findings.description: Reflected XSS: parameter 'name' on GET http://127.0.0.1:5050/file?name=%3Cscript%3Ealert%28%27vsxfdb10f2b%27%29%3C%2Fscript%3E reflects the supplied payload in the response without proper encoding. An attacker can execute JavaScript in the victim's browser context.
findings.remediation: Apply context-aware output encoding (HTML, attribute, JavaScript, URL). Use a strict Content-Security-Policy. Validate and reject input containing HTML/JS where it is not expected. Frameworks like React/Angular auto-escape - avoid using innerHTML / dangerouslySetInnerHTML.

findings.name: Reflected XSS
findings.description: Reflected XSS: parameter 'msg' on POST http://127.0.0.1:5050/comment reflects the supplied payload in the response without proper encoding. An attacker can execute JavaScript in the victim's browser context.
findings.evidence:
Comments

test
' OR 1=1--
' OR '1'='1
' OR '1'='1'--
' OR '1'='1'/*
" OR "1"="1
" OR "1"="1"--
') OR ('1'='1
') OR ('1'='1'--
admin'--
admin' #
admin'/*
' UNION SELECT NULL--
' UNION SELECT NULL,NULL--
' UNION SELECT NULL,NULL,NULL--
' AND SLEEP(2)--
"; DROP TABLE users--
1' AND 1=1--
1' AND 1=2--
1 OR 1=1
1) OR 1=1--
' OR '1'='1
' AND '1'='2
findings.remediation: Apply context-aware output encoding (HTML, attribute, JavaScript, URL). Use a strict Content-Security-Policy. Validate and reject input containing HTML/JS where it is not expected. Frameworks like React/Angular auto-escape - avoid using innerHTML / dangerouslySetInnerHTML.

findings.name: Missing CSRF token
findings.description: The POST form on http://127.0.0.1:5050/ (action=http://127.0.0.1:5050/comment) does not contain a recognizable anti-CSRF token. An attacker can trick an authenticated user into submitting this form via a cross-origin request.
findings.remediation: Add an unpredictable, per-session anti-CSRF token to all state-changing forms (typically a hidden input plus a server-side verification step). Use the SameSite=Lax/Strict cookie attribute. For APIs, require a custom header that browsers will not send cross-origin without a preflight.

findings.name: Missing CSRF token
findings.description: The POST form on http://127.0.0.1:5050/ (action=http://127.0.0.1:5050/transfer) does not contain a recognizable anti-CSRF token. An attacker can trick an authenticated user into submitting this form via a cross-origin request.
findings.remediation: Add an unpredictable, per-session anti-CSRF token to all state-changing forms (typically a hidden input plus a server-side verification step). Use the SameSite=Lax/Strict cookie attribute. For APIs, require a custom header that browsers will not send cross-origin without a preflight.

findings.name: Directory Traversal
findings.description: Path traversal via query parameter 'name'. Linux /etc/passwd content detected.
findings.remediation: Never pass user input directly to file system APIs. Resolve and canonicalize paths, then verify they remain inside an allowlisted base directory. Reject input containing '..', NUL bytes, or absolute paths. Run the application with least-privilege FS access.

findings.name: Missing header: X-Frame-Options
findings.description: Response is missing the 'X-Frame-Options' header. Prevents the page from being framed by other origins (clickjacking).
findings.evidence: Response headers: {'Server': 'Werkzeug/3.1.8 Python/3.12.8', 'Date': 'Wed, 29 Apr 2026 05:43:07 GMT', 'Content-Type': 'text/html; charset=utf-8', 'Content-Length': '343', 'Connection': 'close'}
findings.remediation: Set to 'SAMEORIGIN' or 'DENY', or rely on a 'frame-ancestors' CSP directive.

findings.name: Missing header: Content-Security-Policy
findings.description: Response is missing the 'Content-Security-Policy' header. Restricts resource origins and is the strongest defense against XSS.
findings.evidence: Response headers: {'Server': 'Werkzeug/3.1.8 Python/3.12.8', 'Date': 'Wed, 29 Apr 2026 05:43:07 GMT', 'Content-Type': 'text/html; charset=utf-8', 'Content-Length': '343', 'Connection': 'close'}
findings.remediation: Define a strict CSP appropriate for your app; start with 'default-src self'.

findings.name: Missing header: X-XSS-Protection
findings.description: Response is missing the 'X-XSS-Protection' header. Legacy browser XSS filter. Modern browsers ignore it but it is still checked by compliance tooling.
findings.evidence: Response headers: {'Server': 'Werkzeug/3.1.8 Python/3.12.8', 'Date': 'Wed, 29 Apr 2026 05:43:07 GMT', 'Content-Type': 'text/html; charset=utf-8', 'Content-Length': '343', 'Connection': 'close'}
findings.remediation: Set to '0' if you have a strong CSP, or '1; mode=block' for legacy clients.

findings.name: Missing header: Strict-Transport-Security
findings.description: Response is missing the 'Strict-Transport-Security' header. Forces browsers to use HTTPS for the configured domain.
findings.evidence: Response headers: {'Server': 'Werkzeug/3.1.8 Python/3.12.8', 'Date': 'Wed, 29 Apr 2026 05:43:07 GMT', 'Content-Type': 'text/html; charset=utf-8', 'Content-Length': '343', 'Connection': 'close'}
findings.remediation: Set 'max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload' on HTTPS endpoints.

findings.name: Missing header: X-Content-Type-Options
findings.description: Response is missing the 'X-Content-Type-Options' header. Disables MIME-type sniffing.
findings.evidence: Response headers: {'Server': 'Werkzeug/3.1.8 Python/3.12.8', 'Date': 'Wed, 29 Apr 2026 05:43:07 GMT', 'Content-Type': 'text/html; charset=utf-8', 'Content-Length': '343', 'Connection': 'close'}

findings.name: Missing header: Referrer-Policy
findings.description: Response is missing the 'Referrer-Policy' header. Controls how much referrer information is leaked.
findings.evidence: Response headers: {'Server': 'Werkzeug/3.1.8 Python/3.12.8', 'Date': 'Wed, 29 Apr 2026 05:43:07 GMT', 'Content-Type': 'text/html; charset=utf-8', 'Content-Length': '343', 'Connection': 'close'}

findings.name: Missing header: Permissions-Policy
findings.description: Response is missing the 'Permissions-Policy' header. Restricts browser features the page can use.
findings.evidence: Response headers: {'Server': 'Werkzeug/3.1.8 Python/3.12.8', 'Date': 'Wed, 29 Apr 2026 05:43:07 GMT', 'Content-Type': 'text/html; charset=utf-8', 'Content-Length': '343', 'Connection': 'close'}
findings.remediation: Define a Permissions-Policy that disables unused features (camera, geolocation, etc.).

findings.name: Sensitive resource exposed: /robots.txt
findings.description: The path /robots.txt is publicly accessible (HTTP 200) and may leak configuration, credentials, version control metadata, or other sensitive information.
findings.remediation: Restrict access to administrative, configuration, and version control artifacts. Block or 404 these paths at the web server / load balancer level. Audit deployments to ensure backup and dev files are never shipped to production.

findings.name: Sensitive resource exposed: /.env
findings.description: The path /.env is publicly accessible (HTTP 200) and may leak configuration, credentials, version control metadata, or other sensitive information.
findings.remediation: Restrict access to administrative, configuration, and version control artifacts. Block or 404 these paths at the web server / load balancer level. Audit deployments to ensure backup and dev files are never shipped to production.

disclaimer: VulnScan Pro is intended for AUTHORIZED security testing only. Running this tool against systems you do not own or have explicit written permission to test is illegal in most jurisdictions.
